title: Samsung signals record Q2 as AI memory shortage lifts Korean chipmakers past $1T
subtitle: Ahead of Tuesday's earnings, Samsung is guiding to an 18-fold operating profit jump while SK Hynix files for a roughly $28 billion US listing — the largest US debut since SpaceX.
author: AESOP AI Engine
body: Samsung Electronics guided ahead of Tuesday's Q2 earnings release toward record quarterly profits, with local reporting framing the guidance as an 18-fold jump in operating profit driven by AI memory demand. Device Solutions Division president Kim Yong-kwan said the company expects to meet market consensus. Separately, SK Hynix filed for a roughly 28 billion US dollar US stock-market listing — the largest US market debut since SpaceX, and a bid to widen its investor base as high-bandwidth memory becomes the bottleneck constraint on frontier AI training. Combined operating profit at the two Korean chipmakers is projected to approach 150 trillion won for the quarter, a first.

The mechanism behind the numbers is HBM allocation. High-bandwidth memory stacks are the scarce input in Blackwell-class and MI450-class systems — GPUs sit idle without them, and every hyperscaler is queued for the next allocation round. SK Hynix holds the largest share of qualified HBM3E and HBM4 supply and reports Q2 on July 29; Samsung is closing a qualification gap that cost it market share in 2025 and is now shipping into Nvidia's most demanding SKUs. The rally reflects that: Samsung, SK Hynix, and Micron shares are up roughly 158, 273, and 242 percent year-to-date, and all three carry market caps above one trillion dollars.

This is what the AI capex cycle looks like from the supply side. Alphabet, Meta, Microsoft, and Amazon booked more than seven hundred billion dollars of combined capex through Q1 alone, and that capex flows first to TSMC for logic and then to the Korean memory makers for HBM. What was a commodity DRAM industry as recently as 2023 has become a duopoly on the input that gates every model training run above a certain scale. SK Hynix listing in the US is the finance-side move that follows: bring the capital markets closer to the customers, price the shares against the AI trade rather than the Kospi consumer-cycle multiple.

A takeaway for learners: when you read that a lab "can't get compute," the practical constraint is usually memory, not silicon logic. Understanding the difference between HBM3E, HBM4, and the roadmap to HBM4E is more useful than memorizing GPU model names — it is the layer of the stack where the 2026 supply crunch actually lives, and where the profit is being captured. If your career sits anywhere near infrastructure economics, the Korean memory duopoly is worth watching more closely than the next model release.

sources.title: Samsung signals record earnings as AI chip boom could lift quarterly profit past Nvidia — Korea JoongAng Daily
Samsung likely to post 18-fold jump in profit on surging AI demand for memory — New Straits Times
SK Hynix's $28B US Listing Rides AI Memory Supercycle — HeyGoTrade
